comment.body:
so this is OpEx today, 
we have AM and PM expirations as always.
this si bcs the SPX options are european style contracts, so they can be only exercised at expo days.
The AM contracts stopped trading with the thursday close, and the SPXW contracts will trade until today's PM.
The value will be determined this morning at 9.30, thats when the auction starts to calculate the opening index level, determined by aggregating quotations, orders submitted at or just before market open.
This lvl becomes the exercise‐settlement value. This value will be the benchmark to use to settle any ITM options.
For calls it is (Settlement Value – Strike Price)
For puts it is (Strike Price – Settlement Value)
multiplied by 100 (the contract multiplier) 
Then everybody gets his money or not, depending on if his contract expires ITM or not

comment.body:
on 0dte there is not vega hedging. 
i explained it many times: gamma and vol are inverserly correlated. if gamma goes down, vol naturally increases, bcs of how the formula works: if it is not the spot that makes the delta move, then it is vol (vanna). 
or more intuitively: low gamma means less hedging against the move, or even into the direction of the move if gamma is negative.

comment.body:
sum: 
1) Control your emotions—don’t let your emotions control you. Time to grow up, no way around it.
2) Knowledge protects you. It gives you the strength to see through emotional storms (to know when to stop and when to push forward. Stay rational)
3) Only trade with money you’re ready to lose. (Cut the emotional attachment to money)
That’s why algos are better traders than humans. And that’s why the big players are always playing poker with the small ones, they have access to endless metrics.
Our job? Read between the lines, figure out where they’re headed, snatch a little cash from their pockets, and vanish.

comment.body:
This has been discussed multiple times here and everywhere else, and Alma has explained it more than once:
VIX measures the volatility of ATM options with 30 days to expiration..always the ATM strikes, plus the wings with different weightings.
That’s why VIX naturally drops even when the market falls, because on the vol smile, if the strike shifts left (down), IV rises, and if it moves up, IV drops. Fixed-strike vol is what really matters, and from what Alma has written, that’s what she watches.
Just look at the term structure and observe how it moves. Every broker platform provides IV data..find it on yours.
Honestly, I don’t even understand how people trade without knowing these basics.

comment.body:
There’s three kinds of vol. One is realized or historical vol, which is just what already happened, calculated from past data. Then there’s implied vol, which is the vol risk premium in options, or like Alma says, a bet on future volatility. Because of risk/reward, it’s also directly a mirror of the supply/demand ratio. The third one is expected vol, calculated with quant methods, like that Monte Carlo thing Alma posted once, but you don’t need to bother with that.
Alma usually switches between talking about historical vol and implied vol, but historical vol has zero effect on option pricing. Implied vol does. Alma’s said it a bunch of times..if you were paying attention..that IV is a direct input in option pricing formulas. Market makers even call it vega.
So it’s pretty obvious that when she talks about vanna, she means implied vol, ‘cause, once again, historical vol has nothing to do with option pricing or hedging. It’s just the result of price moves.

comment.body:
Fixed strike vol is the real fear index. Vol traders know this. Cem Karsan was the one who brought it to X.
It’s simple..you take today’s ATM IV and check what the same strike was trading at yesterday or a week ago. You can look at different expiries, but the key thing is that it’s the same strike, which is why it’s called fixed strike.
This shows how the vol profile has shifted. Alma’s chart on this is straight-up genius. Take a few minutes and study it

Gives a way more complete picture of vol

comment.body:
It's always on the vol smile. Look..let’s say today’s ATM strike is 100, and its IV is 20%. Yesterday, the market was at 90, and the 100 strike OTM call had an 18% IV.If the market moved up, that same contract..which was an OTM call yesterday at 18% IV..is now ATM at 20% IV. That means vol increased as we moved higher.
That’s all fixed strike vol is..
📌 Check today’s ATM strike IV
📌 Compare it to the same strike’s IV yesterday
